Chief Complaint: Persistent cough and shortness of breath

History of Present Illness:
45-year-old male with type 2 diabetes (ICD-10: E11.65) and hypertension (ICD-10: I10) presents with fever of 102°F, productive cough for 2 weeks, and dyspnea on exertion. Patient reports chest pain and fatigue. Denies hemoptysis.

Medical History:
- Diabetes Mellitus Type 2 (E11.65)
- Hypertension (I10)
- Hyperlipidemia

Current Medications:
- Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Lisinopril 10mg once daily
- Atorvastatin 20mg at bedtime
- Aspirin 81mg daily

Recent Lab Values:
- Blood Glucose: 185 mg/dL
- Systolic BP: 145 mmHg
- WBC: 12.5 units

Assessment:
1. Community-acquired pneumonia (ICD-10: J18.9)
2. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus with complications

Plan:
- Start Amoxicillin 500mg three times daily for 7 days
- Chest X-ray to rule out complications
- Follow-up in 1 week

Chief Complaint: Follow-up for depression and anxiety

History of Present Illness:
52-year-old female with major depressive disorder (ICD-10: F32.9) and generalized anxiety disorder (ICD-10: F41.1) reports improved mood on current regimen. Sleep quality improving. No suicidal ideation.

Current Medications:
- Sertraline 100mg daily
- Alprazolam 0.5mg twice daily as needed
- Vitamin B12 supplement 1000 mcg daily

Recent Lab Values (Jan 10, 2024):
- Thyroid TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal)
- Vitamin B12: 450 pg/mL (normal)

Assessment:
Major depressive disorder (F32.9) - improving
Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1) - stable

Plan:
Continue current medications
Weekly psychotherapy
Recheck labs in 3 months

Chief Complaint: Chest pain on exertion

History of Present Illness:
68-year-old male with history of myocardial infarction (ICD-10: I21.0) and coronary artery disease presents with acute chest pain that radiates to left arm. Pain started 30 minutes ago. Associated with shortness of breath and diaphoresis.

Medical History:
- Acute Myocardial Infarction (I21.0)
- Coronary Artery Disease (I25.1)
- Hypertension (I10)
- Diabetes Type 2 (E11.9)

Current Medications:
- Clopidogrel (Plavix) 75mg daily
- Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Lisinopril 20mg daily
- Atorvastatin 80mg daily
- Aspirin 325mg daily

Vital Signs:
- BP: 165 mmHg systolic
- Heart Rate: 108 bpm
- Respiratory Rate: 22
- Temperature: 98.6°F

Assessment:
1. Acute coronary syndrome (ICD-10: I24.8) - EMERGENCY
2. History of myocardial infarction

Plan:
- EMERGENCY: Transfer to CCU
- EKG and Troponin levels stat
- Oxygen therapy
- Continuous cardiac monitoring

Chief Complaint: Pregnancy check-up - 28 weeks gestation

History of Present Illness:
34-year-old gravida 2, para 1 (G2P1) at 28 weeks gestation presents for routine prenatal visit. Reports feeling baby movements. No complications noted. Blood pressure normal.

Obstetric History:
- Previous pregnancy uncomplicated, normal vaginal delivery 2 years ago

Prenatal Labs (most recent):
- Glucose tolerance test: 98 mg/dL (normal)
- Complete Blood Count: normal
- Blood type: O positive

Current Medications:
- Prenatal vitamins with folic acid 400 mcg daily
- Calcium supplement 600mg twice daily

Assessment:
Normal pregnancy at 28 weeks gestation

Plan:
- Continue prenatal vitamins
- Schedule fetal monitoring at 30 weeks
- Repeat blood pressure check at next visit in 2 weeks
- Patient education on labor signs

Chief Complaint: Chronic pain management follow-up

History of Present Illness:
72-year-old male with chronic lower back pain (ICD-10: M54.5) due to lumbar degenerative disc disease presents for medication adjustment. Current pain level 6/10. Physical therapy helping but wants pain control optimization.

Medical History:
- Chronic lower back pain (M54.5)
- Lumbar degenerative disc disease (M47.27)
- Osteoarthritis (M19.90)

Current Medications:
- Gabapentin 300mg three times daily
- Ibuprofen 400mg four times daily as needed
- Tramadol 50mg once daily at bedtime

Assessment:
Chronic lower back pain - inadequately controlled
Lumbar degenerative disc disease

Plan:
- Increase Gabapentin to 400mg three times daily
- Refer to pain management specialist
- Continue physical therapy
- Consider acupuncture
- Follow-up in 4 weeks
